title: Reconciliation control and approval workflow recovery
business_context: A fictional reconciliation workflow shows mismatched ledger totals while an approval window is approaching.
initial_stakes: A control break could allow incomplete reconciliation evidence into an approval workflow.

turns.situation: Ledger totals and settlement summaries diverge inside the approval window.

options.label: Freeze approval workflow and preserve evidence
options.description: Stop approvals while isolating the reconciliation boundary.
options.expected_outcome: The control break is contained before incomplete evidence is approved.

options.label: Approve using the latest available total
options.description: Continue the workflow before resolving the mismatch.
options.expected_outcome: An unsupported total enters the approval chain.

turns.situation: The mismatch is isolated to a bounded settlement batch and its downstream ledger entries.

options.label: Correlate batch, ledger, and payment evidence
options.description: Trace the mismatch across the affected control chain.
options.expected_outcome: The team identifies the exact reconciliation gap.

options.label: Rebuild the full ledger period
options.description: Expand recovery beyond the isolated batch.
options.expected_outcome: The control window widens and evidence becomes harder to compare.

turns.situation: The control owner needs a recovery decision and independent validation owner.

options.label: Escalate with separated recovery and approval owners
options.description: Assign distinct accountability for correction and control validation.
options.expected_outcome: The approval workflow retains independent oversight.

options.label: Let the recovery owner approve the result

turns.situation: The affected batch is corrected and reconciliation evidence must be retested.

options.label: Re-run reconciliation and compare control totals
options.description: Validate source, ledger, and settlement totals before approval.
options.expected_outcome: The corrected batch passes an evidence-based control check.

options.label: Use the corrected ledger total alone
options.description: Skip independent source and settlement comparison.
options.expected_outcome: The control remains incomplete despite a plausible ledger total.

turns.situation: All control totals agree and the approval workflow can resume.

options.label: Resume approvals with a monitored control checkpoint
options.description: Restore the workflow and verify the next reconciliation cycle.
options.expected_outcome: Approvals resume with evidence that the control remains stable.

options.label: Remove the temporary control immediately
options.description: Return to baseline without a monitoring checkpoint.
options.expected_outcome: The workflow resumes without proving continued control effectiveness.

failure_modes: Expand recovery beyond the fault boundary
